segments: حَدَّثَنِي بَكْرٌ الْأَرْقَطُ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ أَوْ عَنْ شُعَيْبٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) أَنَّهُ دَخَلَ عَلَيْهِ وَاحِدٌ فَقَالَ أَصْلَحَكَ اللَّهُ إِنِّي رَجُلٌ مُنْقَطِعٌ إِلَيْكُمْ بِمَوَدَّتِي وَ قَدْ أَصَابَتْنِي حَاجَةٌ شَدِيدَةٌ وَ قَدْ تَقَرَّبْتُ بِذَلِكَ إِلَى أَهْلِ بَيْتِي وَ قَوْمِي فَلَمْ يَزِدْنِي بِذَلِكَ مِنْهُمْ إِلَّا بُعْداً قَالَ فَمَا آتَاكَ اللَّهُ خَيْرٌ مِمَّا أَخَذَ مِنْكَ

قَالَ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ ادْعُ اللَّهَ لِي أَنْ يُغْنِيَنِي عَنْ خَلْقِهِ قَالَ إِنَّ اللَّهَ قَسَّمَ رِزْقَ مَنْ شَاءَ عَلَى يَدَيْ مَنْ شَاءَ وَ لَكِنْ سَلِ اللَّهَ أَنْ يُغْنِيَكَ عَنِ الْحَاجَةِ الَّتِي تَضْطَرُّكَ إِلَى لِئَامِ خَلْقِهِ .
arabic.text:
حَدَّثَنِي بَكْرٌ الْأَرْقَطُ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ أَوْ عَنْ شُعَيْبٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) أَنَّهُ دَخَلَ عَلَيْهِ وَاحِدٌ فَقَالَ أَصْلَحَكَ اللَّهُ إِنِّي رَجُلٌ مُنْقَطِعٌ إِلَيْكُمْ بِمَوَدَّتِي وَ قَدْ أَصَابَتْنِي حَاجَةٌ شَدِيدَةٌ وَ قَدْ تَقَرَّبْتُ بِذَلِكَ إِلَى أَهْلِ بَيْتِي وَ قَوْمِي فَلَمْ يَزِدْنِي بِذَلِكَ مِنْهُمْ إِلَّا بُعْداً قَالَ فَمَا آتَاكَ اللَّهُ خَيْرٌ مِمَّا أَخَذَ مِنْكَ

قَالَ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ ادْعُ اللَّهَ لِي أَنْ يُغْنِيَنِي عَنْ خَلْقِهِ قَالَ إِنَّ اللَّهَ قَسَّمَ رِزْقَ مَنْ شَاءَ عَلَى يَدَيْ مَنْ شَاءَ وَ لَكِنْ سَلِ اللَّهَ أَنْ يُغْنِيَكَ عَنِ الْحَاجَةِ الَّتِي تَضْطَرُّكَ إِلَى لِئَامِ خَلْقِهِ .

segments: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Muhammad Bin Sinan, from Aban Bin Abdul Malik who said, ‘Bakr Al Arqat narrated to me, from Abu Abdullah,

(It has been narrated) from Shuayb, from Abu Abdullah<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup>, that he went over to him<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> alone and he said, ‘May Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> Keep you<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> well! I am a man who has been cut-off to you<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> due to my cordiality and I have been hit by a severe need. I had approached with that to my family and my people but that did not increase me from them except for the distance’. He<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> said: ‘So whatever Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> has Given you is better than what He<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> has Taken from you’.

He said, ‘May I be sacrificed for you<sup>asws</sup>! Supplicate for me that He<sup>azwj</sup> Makes me to be needless from His<sup>azwj</sup> creatures’. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Distributes sustenance to whoever He<sup>azwj</sup> so Desires to, upon the hands of the ones whom He<sup>azwj</sup> so Desires to. But, ask Allah<sup>azwj</sup> that He<sup>azwj</sup> Makes you to be self-sufficient from the needs which are making you desperate to the ignoble ones of His<sup>azwj</sup> creatures’.
en.hubeali.text:
Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Muhammad Bin Sinan, from Aban Bin Abdul Malik who said, ‘Bakr Al Arqat narrated to me, from Abu Abdullah,

(It has been narrated) from Shuayb, from Abu Abdullah<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup>, that he went over to him<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> alone and he said, ‘May Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> Keep you<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> well! I am a man who has been cut-off to you<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> due to my cordiality and I have been hit by a severe need. I had approached with that to my family and my people but that did not increase me from them except for the distance’. He<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> said: ‘So whatever Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> has Given you is better than what He<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> has Taken from you’.

He said, ‘May I be sacrificed for you<sup>asws</sup>! Supplicate for me that He<sup>azwj</sup> Makes me to be needless from His<sup>azwj</sup> creatures’. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Distributes sustenance to whoever He<sup>azwj</sup> so Desires to, upon the hands of the ones whom He<sup>azwj</sup> so Desires to. But, ask Allah<sup>azwj</sup> that He<sup>azwj</sup> Makes you to be self-sufficient from the needs which are making you desperate to the ignoble ones of His<sup>azwj</sup> creatures’.

segments: 1. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Muhammad ibn Sinan from Aban ibn ‘Abd al-Malik who has said that narrated to him Bakr ibn Arqat from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) or from Shu’ayb from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “Once someone came to abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) and said, ‘May Allah keep you well, I am a man who devoutly loves you. I face a pressing need. I sought help from my family and tribe but it only has distanced me from them.’ The Imam said, ‘What Allah has given you is better than what He has taken from you.’ The man said, ‘May Allah keep my soul in service for your cause, pray for me to Allah so He will make me independent of His creatures.’ The Imam said, ‘Allah has assigned the sustenance of whomever He wanted to come through the hands of whomever He wanted, however, you can ask Allah to make you free of such needs that may force you to ask for help the ignoble ones of His creatures.’”
en.sarwar.text: 1. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Muhammad ibn Sinan from Aban ibn ‘Abd al-Malik who has said that narrated to him Bakr ibn Arqat from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) or from Shu’ayb from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “Once someone came to abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) and said, ‘May Allah keep you well, I am a man who devoutly loves you. I face a pressing need. I sought help from my family and tribe but it only has distanced me from them.’ The Imam said, ‘What Allah has given you is better than what He has taken from you.’ The man said, ‘May Allah keep my soul in service for your cause, pray for me to Allah so He will make me independent of His creatures.’ The Imam said, ‘Allah has assigned the sustenance of whomever He wanted to come through the hands of whomever He wanted, however, you can ask Allah to make you free of such needs that may force you to ask for help the ignoble ones of His creatures.’”

gradings.raw: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/374)
gradings.label: ضعيف
gradings.text: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/374)

narratorChain.raw: مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ يَحْيَى عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنْ أَبَانِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ قَالَ

segments: الْفَقْرُ الْمَوْتُ الْأَحْمَرُ فَقُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) الْفَقْرُ مِنَ الدِّينَارِ وَ الدِّرْهَمِ فَقَالَ لَا وَ لَكِنْ مِنَ الدِّين .ِ
arabic.text: الْفَقْرُ الْمَوْتُ الْأَحْمَرُ فَقُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) الْفَقْرُ مِنَ الدِّينَارِ وَ الدِّرْهَمِ فَقَالَ لَا وَ لَكِنْ مِنَ الدِّين .ِ

segments: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Ali Bin Asbat, from the one who mentioned it,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The poverty is the red death’. So I said to Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, ‘(Is it) the poverty from the Dinars and the Dirhams?’ So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘No, but from the Religion’.
en.hubeali.text:
A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Ali Bin Asbat, from the one who mentioned it,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The poverty is the red death’. So I said to Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, ‘(Is it) the poverty from the Dinars and the Dirhams?’ So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘No, but from the Religion’.

segments: 2. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from Ali ibn Asbat from those he has mentioned (in his book) from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) has said, ‘Poverty is a painful death.’ I (the narrator) then asked him, ‘Is it poverty due to deficiency of Dirham and Dinar (units of money)?’ The Imam said, ‘No, in fact, it is poverty due to deficiency in religion.’”
en.sarwar.text: 2. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from Ali ibn Asbat from those he has mentioned (in his book) from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) has said, ‘Poverty is a painful death.’ I (the narrator) then asked him, ‘Is it poverty due to deficiency of Dirham and Dinar (units of money)?’ The Imam said, ‘No, in fact, it is poverty due to deficiency in religion.’”

narratorChain.raw: عِدَّةٌ مِنْ أَصْحَابِنَا عَنْ سَهْلِ بْنِ زِيَادٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَسْبَاطٍ عَمَّنْ ذَكَرَهُ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) قَالَ
